title: ATF — Weapons Trace Records and Investigative Files — North Hollywood Shootout — February 28, 1997

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: ATF — Weapons Trace Records and Investigative Files — North Hollywood Shootout — February 28, 1997
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am writing to request records pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552.
I request all records of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives relating to:
1. All weapons trace requests submitted in connection with the bank robbery and officer-involved shooting that occurred on February 28, 1997, at Bank of America Branch 384, 6600 Laurel Canyon Boulevard, North Hollywood, California. This includes eTrace records, Firearms Tracing System (FTS) records, and any correspondence with the Los Angeles Police Department or any other law enforcement agency arising from trace requests related to this incident.
2. All ATF records relating to Larry Eugene Phillips Jr., date of birth September 20, 1970, also known as Larry Eugene Warfel, who was killed at the above location on February 28, 1997.
3. All records relating to Emil Decebal Matasareanu, date of birth July 19, 1968, who was killed at the above location on February 28, 1997.
4. Any ATF investigative report, field report, or correspondence relating to the modification or conversion of the firearms used in the February 28, 1997 North Hollywood incident, including but not limited to any assessment of whether those firearms were illegally converted to fire automatically or near-automatically.
5. Any correspondence between ATF and the Los Angeles Police Department, the Los Angeles District Attorney's Office, or the Federal Bureau of Investigation relating to the firearms, suspects, or investigation described above.
Both subjects named above are deceased. Both died on February 28, 1997. Privacy Act and FOIA personal privacy exemptions are therefore significantly reduced for these records.
I request that any reasonably segregable portion of any responsive record be provided even if other portions are withheld. Please identify the specific exemption claimed for any withheld material.
Fee Waiver Request
I request a full waiver of search, duplication, and review fees pursuant to 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(A)(iii). I am a journalist and author researching a book on the 1997 North Hollywood shootout for public interest publication. Disclosure of these records is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of ATF's role in the investigation of the weapons used in one of the most significant armed confrontations in American law enforcement history. The requested records are not sought for commercial use.
If a fee waiver is not granted, I request to be notified before any fees exceeding $25.00 are incurred, and I agree to pay reasonable fees up to that amount without prior notification.
Format
I request records in electronic format where available. If records exist only in paper form, I request copies.
If any portion of this request is denied, I request an itemized index of withheld material pursuant to Vaughn v. Rosen, 484 F.2d 820 (D.C. Cir. 1973).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Daniel Brown

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Please provide any and all of the the following  that pertain to  the 2021-22, 2022-23, 2023-24, 2024-25, and/or 2025-26 academic years—

                    1.	Any and all records reflecting data bearing on students’ social, emotional, or psychological well-being, disaggregated by race/ethnicity where available, for the District as a whole and for each of the District’s high schools including—
                                    a.	Youth Truth Survey results;
                                    b.	California Healthy Kids Survey results (CHKS); and
                                     c.	 Any other Districtwide or school-site-specific surveys or measures.

                    2.	Any and all records reflecting annual and/or semi-annual academic data, disaggregated by race/ethnicity where available, for the District as a whole and for each of the District’s high schools including—
                                    a.	DFI Semester Grades;
                                    b.	Local Control and Accountability Plans (LCAP);
                                     c.	School Accountability Report Cards (SARC); and
                                    d.	Any other Districtwide or school-site-specific data that is or can be disaggregated by race.

                    3.	Any and all reports, records, and analyses created by, for, or at the direction of the District pertaining to any of the aforementioned data.

Where school-specific data is disaggregated by grade, please provide combined data for all grades as well where available; our goal with this request is to ensure that racial/ethnic groups whose numbers are too small to be reportable at the grade level will reach reportable numbers when all grade levels are included in the data for a given school year.

Please provide any raw data in excel or csv format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

Please provide all records, in any format (including audio, video, written, electronic, and digital records) relating to 911 calls, non-emergency calls, dispatch communications, or other police activity involving phone number 720-799-5294 and/or the address 2362 Shenandoah Rd, Palm Bay, FL 32905, occurring between September 9, 2023 and September 14, 2023.

This request includes records received, logged, transferred, handled, generated, or referenced by your agency, regardless of whether a police response ultimately occurred.

Specifically, please include:

1. Phone Call Recordings

Audio recordings of all 911 calls placed from 720-799-5294 during the specified time period

Audio recordings of non-emergency calls placed to or received by your agency from 720-799-5294

Audio recordings of any transferred calls or call-backs between dispatch, officers, or other agencies

2. Call Logs & Metadata

Call logs showing the date, time, and duration of each call

Call-taker or dispatcher notes

Call classification and disposition codes

Incident, event, or case numbers associated with each call

3. Dispatch & CAD Records

All CAD entries and calls for service associated with phone number 720-799-5294

All CAD entries and calls for service associated with the address 2362 Shenandoah Rd, Palm Bay, FL 32905 during the requested period

Dispatch notes and time-stamped activity logs

Unit assignments, dispatch decisions, and response actions

4. Radio & Dispatch Communications

Audio recordings of dispatch radio traffic and unit-to-unit radio communications related to these calls or the address

5. Body Camera & Dash Camera Footage

All body-worn camera footage and dash camera footage associated with CAD calls or calls for service involving the phone number or address during the specified time period

6. Related Reports

Any incident reports, offense reports, or supplemental reports generated as a result of these calls or calls for service

Please include all responsive records regardless of whether an officer was dispatched, an arrest occurred, or a report was finalized.

If any records are withheld or redacted, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon and provide the non-exempt portions.

If any responsive records have been destroyed, lost, or no longer exist, please provide documentation reflecting that fact.

Please provide records in electronic format where available.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Ashley Kautz

title: EscambiaVotes GIS Mapping Data & IT Administration (2022-Present)

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: EscambiaVotes GIS Mapping Data & IT Administration (2022-Present)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

To the Public Records Custodian / Hon. Robert Bender, Escambia County Supervisor of Elections:

Under the Florida Sunshine Law (Chapter 119, F.S.), I am requesting access to and copies of the following public records regarding the public-facing election results portal hosted on or linked via EscambiaVotes.gov.

Publicly available data on the EscambiaVotes portal indicates a systemic, multi-year degradation of the GIS precinct mapping module. Specifically, the public map functioned correctly during the 2022 General Election (last updated 12/8/2022). However, it suffered a complete failure during the 2024 Primary and General Elections (displaying no map data), and remains only partially functional as of the 2025 Special Election (displaying broken or unlinked shapefiles).

Because the underlying tabular voting data is actively present on the site, this request seeks to audit the administrative failure of the GIS mapping API and the visual transparency tool itself. I am requesting the following records:

1. IT and Administrative Correspondence (Dec 2022 - Present):
Any internal IT helpdesk tickets, error logs, or administrative memorandums generated between December 8, 2022, and the present date regarding GIS shapefile errors, precinct redistricting desynchronization, or the failure of the mapping API to visually populate precinct data on the public portal.

2. Third-Party Vendor Communications:
Any communications (including emails, letters, or service requests) between the Escambia County Supervisor of Elections office and the third-party election software vendor/portal host (e.g., VR Systems) dated between December 8, 2022, and the present date regarding the broken map data, API error resolution, or portal maintenance for the 2024 and 2025 elections.

Statutory Compliance:
If you determine that any portion of these requested records is exempt from disclosure under Chapter 119, Florida law requires you to state in writing the basis for the exemption, including the exact statutory citation authorizing the redaction or denial.

Please advise me of any costs associated with fulfilling this request prior to processing.

Thank you for your lawful compliance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Matthew Joseph Luecht

communications.subject: Request Received 2026-NavyFOIA-006381
communications.communication:
Good Day, 

Your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request of March 31, 2026 has been received by the Navy Personnel Command (NPC).  This command received your request on April 1, 2026. Your FOIA request number is 2026-NavyFOIA-006381 and your FOIA correspondence file number is 6NPC0484. 

Due to the government furlough that occurred from 1 October 2025 to 12 November 2025, the Department of the Navy was legally required to suspend all non-excepted operations, including the processing of Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) requests. During this period, FOIA personnel were prohibited from performing their duties, which resulted in unavoidable delays and a temporary halt in responding to pending inquiries. In addition, FOIA analysts who were furloughed lost access to the online Navy FOIA tracking system, Secure Release, until 10 December 2025. Now that normal operations have resumed, the FOIA Office is working diligently to address the backlog created by the furlough, and the holidays and is committed to processing requests as efficiently and transparently as possible. We appreciate the your patience and understanding during this interruption in service. 

The records you seek require a search in and/or consultation with another office, and so your request falls within the "unusual circumstances" See 5 U.S.C. § 552 (a)(6)(B)(i)-(iii).  Because of these unusual circumstances, we may need to extend the time limit to respond to your request beyond the twenty days provided by the statute.  For your information, we use multiple tracks to process requests, but within the tracks we working an agile manner.  The time needed to complete our work on your request will necessarily depend on a variety of factors, including the complexity of a records search, the volume and complexity of any material located, and the order of receipt of your request.  At this time we have assigned your request to the complex track.  Any decision with regard to the application of fees will be made only after we determine whether fees will be implicated for this request. 

If you have questions or concerns my contact information is below. 

Sincerely, 
ERIN J. RAMIL 
Bureau of Naval Personnel 
Office of Legal Counsel 
FOIA/PA Officer, BUPERS-00J6 
erin.j.ramil.civ@us.navy.mil 
(901) 874-3165
